title: comarostaphylis glabrata
intro: Comarostaphylis glabrata, also known as the rough bush, is a flowering plant species in the family Ebenaceae. It is a tree or shrub native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, particularly in Fiji, Samoa, and Tonga. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Comarostaphylis glabrata is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its health benefits.
